label: Nature Flow
description: Organic, eco, and wellness-led landing pages with softer motion and earthy palettes.
signatureMotif: organic curves, earth gradients, and natural contrast in section flow
promptHints: Favor earth-tone contrast, flowing transitions, and softer section rhythm over hard grids.
The page should feel ecological and grounded rather than tech-polished.

layouts: Use a staggered hero with left-aligned serif headline, rounded CTA cluster, and an overlapping leaf-shaped image card drifting into the next section.
Build feature sections as alternating 55/45 text-and-image bands with curved top or bottom dividers that soften each transition.
Compose testimonials or benefits in a loose three-card row with uneven vertical offsets, soft backgrounds, and small botanical icon accents.
Use a product or offering showcase with one large rounded card on one side and two stacked supporting cards on the other.
Arrange the final CTA as a centered invitation block inside a pill-shaped panel, surrounded by generous whitespace and subtle earth-tone glow.
motifs: Use organic section separators, earth-tinted gradients, and soft radial washes that make the page feel like flowing terrain.
Favor large rounded corners, low-contrast borders, and layered cream-green surfaces instead of crisp white cards on hard grid lines.
Pair expressive serif headlines with calm sans body text, letting oversized type and breathing room create a grounded wellness rhythm.
Introduce nature-led imagery, abstract leaf forms, or soft blob backplates that frame content without feeling illustrative or childish.
antiPatterns: Avoid sharp rectilinear dashboards, dense comparison tables, or enterprise-style blocks that make the page feel corporate and stiff.
Never use neon gradients, glassmorphism, or glossy tech UI highlights that fight the earthy and ecological tone.
Avoid perfectly symmetrical section stacking from top to bottom; this variant needs gentle asymmetry and flowing transitions to feel alive.
Do not rely on stark black-and-white contrast or pure white surfaces, because they erase the warm natural softness of the palette.

themeTokens.bodyBackgroundImage: linear-gradient(to bottom, color-mix(in oklab, var(--color-primary) 8%, white) 0%, transparent 28%), radial-gradient(circle at top right, color-mix(in oklab, var(--color-accent) 16%, white) 0%, transparent 22%)
